item.title: Language and vision in conceptual processing: Multilevel analysis and statistical power
item.description:
Study at a glance

graph TD A["Three datasets: semantic priming,
semantic decision, lexical decision"] --> B["Add language-based and
vision-based measures"] B --> C["Mixed-effects models across
individuals, words and tasks"] C --> D["Language-based information
more important than vision-based"] C --> E["Priming: both stronger
when words shown faster"] C --> F["Higher-vocabulary readers show
a task-relevance advantage"] C --> G["Power: 300 participants for
language effects; over 1,000
for vision effects"]

Related podcasts

Created using NotebookLM, with all the benefits and blind spots of human editing.

Reference

Bernabeu, P., Lynott, D., & Connell, L. (2022). Language and vision in conceptual processing: Multilevel analysis and statistical power. OSF. https://osf.io/dnskh

item.title: Preregistration: The interplay between linguistic and embodied systems in conceptual processing
item.description:
Study at a glance

graph TD A["Conceptual processing"] --> B["Linguistic distributional
systems"] A --> C["Embodied systems"] B --> D["Word co-occurrence"] B --> E["Word association"] C --> F["Sensorimotor information"] C --> G["Emotional information"] H["Calgary Semantic Decision
project data"] --> I["Confirmatory
research questions"] B --> I C --> I J["Individual differences
in vocabulary size"] -->|moderates| I

Reference

Bernabeu, P., Lynott, D., & Connell, L. (2021). Preregistration: The interplay between linguistic and embodied systems in conceptual processing. OSF. https://osf.io/ftydw

Related podcast

Created using NotebookLM, with all the benefits and blind spots of human editing.

item.title: Dutch modality exclusivity norms for 336 properties and 411 concepts
item.description:
Study at a glance

graph TD A["42 Dutch speakers"] --> B["Rate auditory, haptic and visual strength
of 336 properties + 411 concepts"] B --> C["Mean ratings per word
(acceptable reliability)"] C --> D["Derived measures:
modality exclusivity, perceptual strength"] C --> E["Linked corpus variables:
length, frequency, distinctiveness,
concreteness, age of acquisition"] D --> F["Replicate Lynott and
Connell (2009, 2013)"] F --> G["Uni-, bi- and tri-modal words;
vision most prevalent"] F --> H["Vision and touch related;
audition more independent"] F --> I["Properties more perceptual
than concepts"] F --> J["Sound symbolism: auditory strength
best predicts lexical properties"]

Reference

Bernabeu, P. (2018). Dutch modality exclusivity norms for 336 properties and 411 concepts. PsyArXiv. https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/s2c5h

Related podcast

Created using NotebookLM, with all the benefits and blind spots of human editing.
